description: Use a compact band of metrics, customer counts, stars, contributors, deploys, or rankings to reinforce credibility after the core product story is established.
evidence_notes: Railway uses large platform counters and deploy totals.
VoidZero uses downloads, stars, and contributor counts.
Notion reinforces scale and adoption through rankings and usage signals.
rules: Place metrics after the core value proposition, not before it.
Choose proof numbers that the audience can interpret quickly.
Use structural or qualitative proof copy until real production metrics exist.
Keep visual treatment simple so numbers feel credible.
implementation_notes: Render as a small grid or strip with strong labels and one short supporting explanation.
Avoid over-animating counters; instant or subtle updates are usually enough.
Validate live-data fallbacks so users never see zeroed or broken proof states.
avoid_when: The proof numbers are weak, stale, or not actually meaningful to the buyer.
The metric strip appears before the user understands the product.
The data source is unreliable or likely to fail at render time.
